file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Shipment Tracking page of the application. Contains all UI element locators for tracking ink/toner cartridge shipments and paper deliveries, FAQ section elements, shipment history table components, and support links. Provides methods to interact with FAQ questions, retrieve page title, and access various tracking-related UI elements. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality and uses ElementsDict for organized locator storage.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and user interactions for the Shipment Tracking page where users can view the status of their ink cartridge and paper shipments. Contains locators for different cartridge types (K, C, M, Y, CMY), paper tracking items, FAQ accordion sections with questions and answers, shipment history table with date/description/tracking number columns, support card elements, and various help links including recycling and missing ink support. Provides methods to retrieve the page title with configurable wait timeout, access specific FAQ questions by index, and dynamically get selectors by name. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns.

locators.ui_element_description: FAQ accordion question buttons in the shipment tracking page. These are clickable button elements that expand/collapse to show answers when clicked.
First FAQ answer section container identified by ID item-0. Contains the expanded answer content for the first FAQ question.
Second FAQ answer section container identified by ID item-1. Contains the expanded answer content for the second FAQ question.
Third FAQ answer section container identified by ID item-2. Contains the expanded answer content for the third FAQ question.
Fourth FAQ answer section container identified by ID item-3. Contains the expanded answer content for the fourth FAQ question.
Tracking card element for Black (K) ink cartridge shipment. Displays tracking information and status for the black cartridge.
Tracking card element for Cyan (C) ink cartridge shipment. Displays tracking information and status for the cyan cartridge.
Tracking card element for Magenta (M) ink cartridge shipment. Displays tracking information and status for the magenta cartridge.
Tracking card element for Yellow (Y) ink cartridge shipment. Displays tracking information and status for the yellow cartridge.
Tracking card element for combined CMY (Cyan, Magenta, Yellow) tri-color ink cartridge shipment. Displays tracking information for the tri-color cartridge.
Paper shipment tracking card container. Displays tracking information and status for paper deliveries in the HP Instant Ink service.
Main page title element for the Shipment Tracking page. Located within the ink-shipment-ucde section and identified by data-testid page-title.
FAQ card container element that holds all frequently asked questions and answers related to ink shipment tracking.
Link within the FAQ section that navigates to the plan overview page. Provides users access to detailed information about their subscription plan.
Link within the FAQ section that navigates to the change/update plan page. Allows users to modify their subscription plan settings.
Recycle information link located within the fourth FAQ answer section (item-3). Provides information about cartridge recycling program.
Support card container for cartridge-related help and support options. Contains links to various cartridge support resources.
Link that navigates to the cartridge recycling page where users can learn about and participate in the HP cartridge recycling program.
Support link for users to report missing or lost ink cartridges. Navigates to a form or support page for missing cartridge issues.
Date column header button in the shipment history table. Likely sortable button that allows users to sort shipment history by date.
Description column in the shipment history table. Displays descriptive text about each shipment event or status update.
Tracking number column in the shipment history table. Contains clickable tracking number links that may open carrier tracking pages.
Dropdown selector control for the shipment history table. Allows users to filter or select different views of shipment history.
Dropdown options list for the history table selector. Contains the available filter or view options for the shipment history table.
Main container element for the shipment history table. Contains all rows and columns showing historical shipment tracking information.
Activity description text elements within the shipment history table. Displays detailed descriptions of shipment activities and status updates.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the ShipmentTrackingPage by storing the Playwright page instance as self.page and creating an ElementsDict instance to store all locators. Then systematically defines 27 UI element locators for the shipment tracking page including FAQ accordion buttons and answer sections (faq_question, faq_answer1-4), individual cartridge tracking cards for different ink colors (kCartridgeItem, cCartridgeItem, mCartridgeItem, yCartridgeItem, cmyCartridgeItem), paper tracking item, page title, FAQ card container, FAQ links (overview, update plan, recycle), cartridge support card, support links (recycle cartridge, missing ink), and shipment history table components (table date, description, tracking number, selector, selector list, table container, history description). All locators are stored as attributes of the self.elements ElementsDict object for organized access throughout the class methods.

parameters.name: wait
parameters.description: Maximum wait time in milliseconds to wait for the page title element to appear. Defaults to 60000ms (60 seconds).

functions.what_it_does: Waits for the page title element to be present in the DOM and returns the Playwright ElementHandle for that element. Uses Playwright's wait_for_selector method on the page object with the page_title selector from self.elements and a configurable timeout specified by the wait parameter (defaulting to 60 seconds). This method is useful for verifying that the shipment tracking page has loaded successfully before proceeding with other interactions. Returns the ElementHandle object which can be used for further operations like getting text content or checking visibility.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: Zero-based index of the FAQ question to retrieve. For example, 0 for the first question, 1 for the second question, etc.

functions.docstring: Returns the locator for a specific FAQ question by index (0-based).
functions.what_it_does: Returns a Playwright Locator object for a specific FAQ question button based on the provided zero-based index. Uses the page.locator method with the faq_question selector from self.elements (which targets all FAQ accordion buttons), then chains the .nth(index) method to select the specific question at the given index position. This allows tests to interact with individual FAQ questions in the accordion, such as clicking to expand a specific question or verifying its text content. The index parameter is zero-based, so index 0 returns the first FAQ question, index 1 returns the second, and so on.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: String name of the selector to retrieve from the elements dictionary. Must match an existing element name like 'page_title', 'faq_question', 'kCartridgeItem', etc.

functions.what_it_does: Dynamically retrieves a selector string from the elements dictionary by name. First checks if the provided name exists as a key in the self.elements dictionary using the 'in' operator. If the name exists, returns the corresponding selector value from self.elements[name]. If the name does not exist in the elements dictionary, raises an AttributeError with a descriptive message indicating that the selector name was not found in this page object class. This method provides a safe way to access selectors dynamically with proper error handling, useful when selector names are determined at runtime or passed as parameters.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This page object follows a clean pattern using ElementsDict for locator storage, which provides a structured way to organize and access selectors. The page contains comprehensive coverage of the shipment tracking UI including multiple cartridge types (K, C, M, Y, CMY), paper tracking, FAQ sections, and shipment history table. The use of data-testid attributes for most locators indicates good test automation practices with stable selectors. The faq_question method demonstrates a useful pattern for accessing multiple similar elements by index. The get_selector method provides dynamic selector access with error handling. This page object has 27 locators covering all major UI components, making it very comprehensive for shipment tracking test scenarios. The class extends BasePageObject which likely provides common page interaction utilities.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Shipping and Billing Management page of the application. Contains all UI element locators for managing shipping addresses and payment methods including shipping address form fields, billing payment form fields, modal dialogs, error messages, and action buttons. Provides a static method for selecting business account type. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality and uses the inherited elements dictionary for locator storage.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates all UI elements and user interactions for the Shipping and Billing Management page where users can add, edit, and manage their shipping addresses and payment methods. Contains locators organized into three main sections: shipping section (manage address link, form modal, address fields, validation messages, save/cancel buttons), billing section (manage payment link, billing form modal, payment method fields, PayPal logo, credit card input, iframe elements), and page-level elements (page title, notification messages, modal close buttons). Provides a static utility method to select business account type radio button. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction patterns and uses the inherited self.elements dictionary for organized locator storage.

locators.ui_element_description: Manage shipping address link/button in the shipping section. Clicking this opens the shipping address form modal for adding or editing the shipping address.
Modal dialog container for the shipping address form. Contains all shipping address input fields and action buttons.
Main container section for shipping address display and management. Shows the current saved shipping address and provides access to edit functionality.
Go back button in the unsupported postal code error modal. Appears when user enters a postal code that is not supported for shipping, allowing them to return and correct the address.
Generic error message display element. Shows validation errors or other error messages related to shipping or billing form submissions.
First name text input field in the shipping address form. User enters their first name for the shipping address.
ZIP code or postal code text input field in the shipping address form. User enters their postal code for address validation and shipping.
Street address line 1 text input field in the shipping address form. Primary street address field for the shipping address.
State or province input field within the shipping address form. Targets the input element inside the state field container.
Radio button or checkbox input for selecting the suggested address option. Appears when address validation suggests a corrected or standardized version of the entered address.
Radio button or checkbox input for selecting the original address as entered by the user. Appears alongside suggested address option when address validation provides alternatives.
Save button in the shipping address form modal. Submits the shipping address form and saves the entered address information.
Confirmation button to ship to the selected or entered address. Final confirmation action after address validation and selection.
Paragraph element displaying the currently saved shipping address. Shows the formatted address text that is currently on file.
Cancel button in the shipping address form modal. Closes the modal and discards any unsaved changes to the shipping address.

Manage payment method link or add billing button in the billing section. Clicking this opens the billing/payment form modal for adding or editing payment methods. Uses a composite selector matching either the manage payment text or add billing button.
Main container section for billing/payment method display and management. Shows the current saved payment method and provides access to edit functionality.
Modal dialog container for the billing/payment form. Contains all payment method input fields and action buttons.
Add payment method button. Opens the billing form modal to add a new payment method when no payment method is currently saved.
Address section within the billing form modal. Contains billing address input fields that may be separate from or linked to the shipping address.
PayPal logo image or icon element. Indicates PayPal as an available payment method option in the billing section.
Main page title element for the Shipping and Billing Management page. Displays the page heading text.
Generic cancel button used in various modals on the page. Closes the modal and discards unsaved changes.
Notification message container that appears after successfully updating shipping or billing information. Displays success confirmation messages to the user.
Close button (typically an X icon) in the modal header. Closes the modal dialog without saving changes.
First address line input field in the billing address section. Primary street address field for billing address entry.
Second address line input field in the billing address section. Optional secondary street address field for apartment, suite, or unit numbers.
Continue button in multi-step forms. Advances to the next step in the billing or shipping address entry process.
Credit card number input field in the billing form. User enters their credit card number for payment method setup.
Inline error message element for billing address validation errors. Displays field-specific error messages below or near the problematic input field.
Error message element for invalid credit card information. Displays when credit card validation fails, showing messages about invalid card number, expired card, or other card-related errors.

Iframe element for secure payment processing or 3D Secure challenge. May contain payment gateway iframe (pgs-iframe) or authentication challenge frame (challengeFrame). Composite selector matches either iframe.
Next or submit button in the payment gateway iframe for adding a credit card. Submits the credit card information to the payment processor.
Radio button to select business account type. Allows user to indicate they are setting up billing for a business account rather than personal account.
Company name text input field. Appears when business account type is selected, allowing user to enter their company or business name.
Icon displaying the detected credit card type (Visa, Mastercard, Amex, etc.). Automatically updates based on the card number entered to show the card brand.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the ShippingBillingPage by first calling the parent BasePageObject constructor using super().__init__(page) to inherit common page functionality and initialize the self.elements dictionary. Then systematically defines 36 UI element locators organized into three main sections. The shipping section includes locators for manage_shipping_address link, shipping_form_modal container, shipping_section display, go_back_unsupported_shipping button, error_message display, form fields (first_name_field, zip_code_field, street1, state), address validation options (suggested_address_option, original_address_option), action buttons (save_button, ship_to_this_address, cancel_address_button), and saved_address display. The billing section includes manage_your_payment_method_link, billing_section container, billing_form_modal, add_payment_method button, address_billing_form_modal, paypal_logo, billing address fields (input_first_address, input_second_address), input_card_number field, error messages (billing_address_error_message, credit_card_invalid_error_message), billing_modal_iframe, billing_next_button, business account fields (business_radio_button, company_name), and card_type_icon. Page-level elements include page_title, cancel_button, update_shipping_billing_message notification, close_modal_button, and continue_button. All locators are stored as attributes of the inherited self.elements object for organized access throughout the class.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance to perform the action on

functions.docstring: Selects the business account type radio button
functions.what_it_does: Selects the business account type radio button on the shipping/billing page. Uses the page.locator method to find the business radio button element using the data-testid selector '[data-testid="business-radio-button"]', then chains .first to get the first matching element (in case multiple exist), and finally calls .click(force=True) to perform a forced click action that bypasses actionability checks. The force=True parameter ensures the click happens even if the element is not in the viewport or is covered by other elements. This is a static method so it can be called without instantiating the ShippingBillingPage class, useful for utility operations or when the page object instance is not available.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This page object is comprehensive and well-organized, covering both shipping and billing management functionality. It contains 36 locators systematically organized by functional area (shipping, billing, page-level). The use of data-testid attributes for most critical elements indicates good test automation practices. The class extends BasePageObject and calls super().__init__(page) which suggests it inherits an elements dictionary and common page methods. The select_business_account_type static method is interesting as it provides a utility function that can be called without instantiating the page object. The page handles complex scenarios including address validation with suggested/original address options, iframe-based payment processing, business vs personal account types, and multiple error message types. The locators cover both modal dialogs and main page content, indicating this page manages multiple UI states and overlays.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Sign In page of the application. Contains basic UI element locators for the sign-in form including email input, password input, and submit buttons. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality. This is a minimal page object focused on authentication form elements.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates UI elements for the application Sign In page. Provides locators for the email/username input field, use password button (likely for switching from other auth methods to password-based auth), password input field, and sign-in submit button. This is a minimal page object focused solely on locator definitions without custom interaction methods, relying on inherited BasePageObject methods for interactions. The import of test_flows_common suggests this page may be used in conjunction with common test flow utilities.

locators.ui_element_description: Email or username text input field on the sign-in form. This is the primary identifier field where users enter their email address or username to begin the authentication process.

Button to proceed with password-based authentication after entering username/email. Likely submits the username form and reveals the password input field, or switches the authentication method to password-based login.
Password input field on the sign-in form. Masked text input where users enter their password for authentication.
Sign in submit button that submits the authentication form with the entered credentials. Final action button to complete the sign-in process.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the SignInPage by first calling the parent BasePageObject constructor using super().__init__(page) to inherit common page functionality and initialize the self.elements dictionary. Then defines four UI element locators for the sign-in form: email_input field using ID selector '#username', use_password_button using ID selector '#user-name-form-submit', password_input field using ID selector '#password', and sign_in_button using ID selector '#sign-in'. All locators are stored as attributes of the inherited self.elements object. This is a minimal constructor focused solely on locator definitions without additional setup logic.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a very minimal page object with only 4 locators and no custom interaction methods beyond the constructor. All locators use simple ID selectors which are generally stable and reliable. The page object relies entirely on inherited methods from BasePageObject for interactions like filling fields and clicking buttons. The import of test_flows_common.test_flows_common as common suggests that sign-in functionality may be implemented as reusable test flows rather than as methods within this page object. This is a common pattern where page objects define locators and common test flows provide the interaction logic. The use_password_button locator suggests a multi-step authentication flow where username is entered first, then a button is clicked to proceed to password entry.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Thank You confirmation page of the application. Contains UI element locators for the thank you confirmation screen that appears after completing a process such as enrollment, purchase, or printer replacement. Includes locators for continue button, thank you card content, email display, cartridge images, subtitle text, and modal close button. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates UI elements for the Thank You confirmation page that appears after users complete various processes like enrollment, subscription setup, or printer replacement. Contains locators for the continue button to proceed to the next step, thank you card displaying confirmation content, close modal button for finishing printer replacement flow, email display showing the user's registered email address, cartridge image showing the subscribed cartridge type, and subtitle text with additional confirmation information. This is a locator-only page object without custom interaction methods, relying on inherited BasePageObject functionality.

locators.ui_element_description: Continue button on the thank you page. Allows user to proceed to the next step or return to the main application after viewing the confirmation message.
Thank you card container displaying the confirmation message and details. Located in the right section of the thank-you-step component, contains the main confirmation content.
Close button in the modal footer for finishing the printer replacement process. Located in the modal footer section, allows user to close the confirmation modal after completing printer replacement.
Email address display element on the thank you page. Shows the user's registered email address as confirmation of where communications will be sent.
Cartridge image element displaying the ink or toner cartridge type that was enrolled or subscribed. Visual confirmation of the cartridge model included in the subscription.
Subtitle text element on the thank you page. Provides additional information or instructions below the main thank you message.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the ThankYouPage by first calling the parent BasePageObject constructor using super().__init__(page) to inherit common page functionality and initialize the self.elements dictionary. Then defines six UI element locators for the thank you confirmation page: continue_button using data-testid selector, thank_you_card using a composite selector targeting the right section within the thank-you-step component, close_modal_finish_printer_replacement using a CSS selector for the modal footer button, email display using a class prefix selector, cartridge_image using a class prefix selector, and subtitle using a class prefix selector. All locators are stored as attributes of the inherited self.elements object for organized access.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is a simple confirmation page object with 6 locators and no custom interaction methods. The locators cover the essential elements of a thank you/confirmation page: action button (continue), content display (thank you card), modal controls (close button), and informational elements (email, cartridge image, subtitle). The use of data-testid for the continue button is good practice, while other elements use class prefix selectors which may be less stable if CSS class names change. The close_modal_finish_printer_replacement locator suggests this page is used in printer replacement workflows. The page object follows the minimal pattern of defining locators only and relying on inherited BasePageObject methods for interactions.

file_analyses.file_purpose: Page object for the Terms of Service HP Smart page. Contains a single UI element locator for the continue button that accepts the terms of service. This is a minimal page object representing a simple consent/agreement screen. Extends BasePageObject for common page functionality.

classes.class_purpose: Encapsulates the UI element for the Terms of Service HP Smart consent page. This is a very minimal page object containing only a single locator for the continue button that users click to accept the terms of service and proceed with the HP Smart application setup or enrollment. The page represents a standard legal consent screen that appears during onboarding or setup flows. Extends BasePageObject to inherit common page interaction methods.

locators.ui_element_description: Continue button in the full-screen consent form footer. This button accepts the HP Smart Terms of Service and allows the user to proceed with the application setup or enrollment process. Located in the footer section of the consent form.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Playwright Page instance representing the browser page

functions.what_it_does: Initializes the TermsOfServiceHPSmartPage by first calling the parent BasePageObject constructor using super().__init__(page) to inherit common page functionality and initialize the self.elements dictionary. Then defines a single UI element locator for the continue button using the ID selector '#full-screen-consent-form-footer-button-continue'. The locator is stored as self.elements.continue_button. This is the simplest page object in the framework with only one locator, reflecting the simple nature of a terms of service consent page that typically has minimal interactive elements.

file_analyses.analysis_notes: This is the simplest page object in batch 15 with only 1 locator and no custom methods. It represents a standard legal consent/terms of service page that appears during onboarding flows. The single continue button uses a descriptive ID selector that clearly indicates its purpose and location (full-screen-consent-form-footer-button-continue). The minimal nature of this page object is appropriate for a simple consent screen that requires only a single user action. The page object follows the framework pattern of extending BasePageObject and using the inherited elements dictionary for locator storage.
